evals.prompt: Use the codex-bridge to implement a Node.js module: create a file `stack.js` that exports a Stack class with push(item), pop(), peek(), and isEmpty() methods. Create `stack.test.js` with tests covering all methods including edge cases (pop on empty stack should throw). Run tests with `node --test`. Use the implement subcommand with --write and --auto-commit targeting /tmp/sspower-eval-1.
evals.expected_output: Structured JSON with status DONE, files_changed including stack.js and stack.test.js, tests.ran=true with 0 failures, and a _commit SHA

assertions.name: returns-valid-json
assertions.text: Bridge stdout is valid JSON parseable output

assertions.name: status-is-done
assertions.text: Response JSON has status field equal to 'DONE'

assertions.name: files-changed-populated
assertions.text: files_changed array contains at least 2 entries (stack.js and stack.test.js)

assertions.name: tests-ran-and-passed
assertions.text: tests.ran is true, tests.failed is 0, tests.passed is > 0

assertions.name: auto-commit-created
assertions.text: _commit field contains a git SHA (40 hex chars)

assertions.name: schema-compliant
assertions.text: Response contains all required fields: status, summary, files_changed, tests, self_review, concerns, questions, blocked_reason

evals.prompt: Use the codex-bridge spec-review subcommand to verify whether an implementation of a Stack class matches a spec requiring push, pop, peek, isEmpty, and a size() getter. The implementation is in /tmp/sspower-eval-1 (from eval 1) and does NOT have a size() method. The spec-review should detect this as non-compliant.
evals.expected_output: Structured JSON with verdict 'non-compliant', at least one entry in the missing array referencing the size() requirement

assertions.name: returns-valid-json
assertions.text: Bridge stdout is valid JSON parseable output

assertions.name: verdict-non-compliant
assertions.text: Response verdict is 'non-compliant' because size() is missing

assertions.name: missing-array-populated
assertions.text: missing array has at least 1 entry mentioning size

assertions.name: schema-compliant
assertions.text: Response contains all required fields: verdict, missing, extra, misunderstandings, summary

evals.prompt: Use the codex-bridge review subcommand to perform a code quality review on the Stack implementation in /tmp/sspower-eval-1. The review should check architecture, tests, error handling, and maintainability.
evals.expected_output: Structured JSON with verdict (approve or needs-attention), strengths array, issues array with severity/title/file/recommendation fields, and an assessment string

assertions.name: returns-valid-json
assertions.text: Bridge stdout is valid JSON parseable output

assertions.name: strengths-populated
assertions.text: strengths array has at least 1 entry

assertions.name: issues-well-structured
assertions.text: If issues exist, each has severity, title, file, and recommendation fields

assertions.name: schema-compliant
assertions.text: Response contains all required fields: verdict, strengths, issues, assessment
